page: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, which contains 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 3780 GB of local disk capacity and is Premium Storage capable. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The AMD EPYC processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptographic accelerations like AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core performance. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Passmark CPU and extended instruction tests also place the processor in the top tier, reflecting high efficiency in vector and integer operations.

Memory performance is highly competitive, with Passmark memory scores and database operations ranking in the top tier, supported by strong uncached read and write memory bandwidth. This high resource density and balanced CPU-to-memory ratio make the instance qualitatively cost-effective for enterprise workloads. It is well-suited for relational databases, Redis deployments, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks. However, for large-scale 70B parameter LLM inference, performance drops to average levels due to the absence of dedicated GPU hardware.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadsv7 family, featuring 64 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 physical cores, it includes 3780 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. The server delivers top-tier single-core performance and strong multi-core capabilities, supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Benchmark results highlight top-tier database operations and memory performance, alongside strong static web serving throughput. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial local disk capacity, this instance is highly suited for database hosting, Redis caching, web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier database and single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for databases and web serving.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated AMD EPYC cores, high-speed local storage, and top-tier database performance.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs with 32 physical cores
256.0 GB of system memory and 3780 GB of local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and database operation benchmark results
Supports AVX-512, vector, and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Optimized for relational databases, Redis caching, and web serving
